questionText: Show that the curve $y = e^{x} + x$ has no stationary points.

steps.explanation: A stationary point exists only where the gradient is zero, so it is enough to show the derivative is never zero.

steps.description: Recall the range of the exponential
steps.explanation: The exponential function is strictly positive everywhere; it never reaches zero or a negative value.
Adding $1$ to a positive quantity gives something greater than $1$, so the gradient is always at least $1$.

steps.description: Deduce the gradient is never zero
steps.explanation: Since the gradient is always strictly positive, it can never equal zero.

steps.description: Confirm by trying to solve the stationary condition
steps.explanation: Setting the gradient to zero would force $e^{x}=-1$.

steps.workingLatex: e^{x} = -1 \text{ has no solution since } e^{x} > 0
steps.explanation: An exponential can never be negative, so no value of $x$ satisfies the stationary condition.

steps.description: Conclude the derivative is never zero
steps.explanation: As the derivative is positive for every $x$, the curve has no point where the gradient is zero.
Therefore $y=e^{x}+x$ has no stationary points; in fact it is strictly increasing everywhere.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: Since $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=e^{x}+1\ge 1>0$ for all $x$, the gradient is never zero, so the curve has no stationary points.
